question: Within this binary totalistic CA family, when does a density-only binomial mean-field model become adequate, and is improvement explained by lattice dimension after neighborhood degree is held fixed?
experimental_unit: one independently seeded toroidal cellular-automaton world

hypotheses.prediction: Mean-field one-step density error and local pair covariance decline with dimension in full Moore neighborhoods as effective degree rises.
hypotheses.falsifier: Neither metric declines, or either reverses consistently, on confirmatory and holdout seeds.

hypotheses.prediction: Holding degree at 12 materially weakens any apparent dimension trend, showing that increased averaging degree rather than dimension alone explains most closure improvement.
hypotheses.falsifier: The degree-matched arm retains a similarly negative error slope and passes the same adequacy boundary as the Moore arm on fresh holdout conditions.

hypotheses.prediction: Literal B3/S23 on growing Moore neighborhoods collapses toward extinction and therefore demonstrates that low prediction error can be trivial rather than structurally rich.
hypotheses.falsifier: Literal B3/S23 sustains nontrivial active regimes at higher dimensions across confirmatory and holdout seeds.

model.update_order: Synchronous totalistic update on a periodic torus; neighbor counts are computed from the entire pre-update state.
model.rng: numpy.default_rng(seed) initializes each world; all later updates are deterministic; shared seeds pair initial random streams only when shapes match.

seed_plan.pairing: Shared initialization stream within equal-shape and equal-density conditions; differing shapes are independent worlds with the same numeric seed, not identical counterfactual states.

denominators.birth_rate: dead cell-ticks whose neighbor count satisfies the birth rule

exposure.eligibility: Every condition constructs its declared toroidal neighborhood and evaluates the declared totalistic rule for every cell-tick.
exposure.activation: Birth and survival masks are evaluated synchronously from pre-update neighbor counts.
exposure.state_change: Per-episode birth, death, survival, and changed-cell receipts are retained.

analysis.uncertainty: Episode-level mean, median, and deterministic bootstrap 95% interval over independent seeds; no tick-level pseudoreplication.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: The three hypotheses and all phase-map cells are reported; adequacy uses a frozen conjunction rather than per-cell significance tests.
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop after all declared episodes, at 1200 wall seconds, above 2048 MB process RSS, above 250 MB retained artifacts, or on failed determinism; do not adapt thresholds after inspecting results.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution; no worker parallelism until RNG and memory isolation are separately established.
